assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and an email address.
No Mollie account, website profile, or API key exists yet.
Test-mode Payments API work is acceptable for first success when that route is selected.

developer_goal: Create a Mollie account and complete the earliest official first paid-test-payment or equivalent first-success path.

first_success.outcome: Unresolved among custom Payments API Checkout paid status, no-code or prebuilt platform plugins, and Mollie Connect platform routes.
first_success.observable_signal: Accepting payments docs show retrieve-payment status paid after Checkout and webhook handling, but Set up Mollie presents multiple peer entry surfaces first.

route_selection.surface: Mollie Set up guides plus account creation and Payments API docs
route_selection.rule: Select the shortest complete account-to-first-success route only after integration surface (API, prebuilt, no-code, Connect) is fixed.

candidates.name: Custom Payments API with Mollie Checkout to status paid
candidates.reason: Official accepting-payments guide demonstrates this programmer path, but Set up Mollie also offers no-code and prebuilt routes that may be shorter for merchants.

candidates.name: Prebuilt ecommerce platform integration
candidates.reason: Set up Mollie presents Shopify, WooCommerce, Magento, and similar plugins as first-class getting-started options with different install actions.

candidates.name: No-code hosted webshop drop-in
candidates.reason: Set up Mollie lists a no-code entry that reaches acceptance without a custom API integration.

candidates.name: Mollie Connect for platforms and marketplaces
candidates.reason: Official setup routes platforms to a separate Connect guide with different account and onboarding semantics.

required_path.action: Create a Mollie account and complete the documented setup information Mollie requires.

required_fields.label: Company, stakeholder, website, bank, and ID details
required_fields.notes: Docs list categories of required information, not a stable control-level field inventory for the live signup form.

required_path.observable_result: The developer can log into the Mollie Web app and continue setup.

required_path.action: Create or select a website profile and obtain a Test API key.

required_fields.label: Website profile
required_fields.notes: Create-an-account mentions website URL and payment-method activation, but exact profile controls before first API call are not fully enumerated.

required_path.observable_result: A bearer Test API key is available for Payments API calls.

required_path.action: For the selected custom API route, create a payment, redirect to Checkout, handle the webhook, and retrieve payment status.
required_path.observable_result: Retrieving the payment returns status paid after checkout completion.

prerequisites.description: Account setup expects a live website, bank account meeting Mollie requirements, and identification documents for legal representatives.

external_gates.description: Verification review may take up to 10 business days and can request more information by email.
Finish setup, ID upload, and bank details are required to receive payouts.

unavoidable_waits.description: Account verification review may take up to 10 business days.
Webhook delivery of payment status may arrive after customer redirect.

platform_outcomes.description: Payments API returns a checkout URL on payment creation.
Mollie hosts Checkout and later notifies the webhook of status changes.

excluded.item: Reading package lists and Help Center eligibility articles
excluded.reason: Documentation reading is not a counted developer action.

excluded.item: Building a fully embedded own-checkout experience
excluded.reason: Accepting payments presents deeper checkout embedding as an optional deeper integration.

uncertainties.question: Which Mollie getting-started surface should define first success: custom Payments API, prebuilt plugin, no-code, or Connect?
uncertainties.impact: Action counts, implementation work, and the observable terminal change across these peer entry points.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A corpus policy choice of one surface, then a fresh walkthrough from account creation to that surface's first success.

uncertainties.question: What exact account and initial-profile states are required before the first Test API call, and can test payments proceed before verification finishes?
uncertainties.impact: Opaque signup and verification gates block verified field counts and may insert waits before API success.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated authenticated walkthrough documenting live signup fields, profile creation, Test API key availability, and whether paid test status is reachable pre-verification.
